title: Mecodium novoguineense
taxonomy: Mecodium novoguineense is a species of fern in the family Hymenophyllaceae. This family is part of the division Pteridophyta, which includes all ferns. The division Pteridophyta is further divided into several classes, and Hymenophyllaceae is classified under the class Hymenophytaceae. Mecodium novoguineense is native to New Guinea and has unique characteristics that set it apart from other fern species. Its classification reflects its evolutionary relationships with other members of the Hymenophyllaceae family.

The health benefits of Mecodium novoguineense include its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in treating various health conditions such as wounds, infections, and oxidative stress.
